details.body: The `--verbose` flag is added unconditionally to every Claude CLI invocation (process_backend.rs:781), which changes behavior on successful invocations. The acceptance criteria explicitly states 'No change to behavior on successful invocations.' With --verbose, Claude CLI writes additional diagnostic output to stderr on every run, increasing memory usage for the captured stderr buffer — potentially significantly for long-running invocations. Consider gating --verbose behind a retry (e.g., only add it when `request.attempt_number > 1`) or behind a configuration flag, so first-attempt successful invocations remain unchanged.

The `error_message` appended to the runtime log in engine.rs:5067 (`error={}`) is unbounded. While process_backend truncates the stderr_tail component to 200 bytes, the full `error.to_string()` includes the error variant preamble, details string, and any wrapped context — which can exceed 500 bytes and will grow further now that diagnostic context is included. Add a truncation cap (e.g., `truncate_str(&error_message, 500)`) to prevent individual runtime log lines from becoming excessively large, especially since the full error is already persisted in journal.ndjson.

src/adapters/process_backend.rs:190-209 and src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5058-5073 still leave the main Claude schema-failure path underdiagnosed. When the Claude envelope parses but `envelope.result` is not valid JSON, the new error only records `result_len`/`stdout_len`; it does not include any `stderr` snippet. Because Claude is now always launched with `--verbose` in src/adapters/process_backend.rs:777-789, the extra diagnostic detail is likely to be emitted on stderr, but that information never reaches the `stage_failed` runtime log for the motivating `invalid Claude result JSON` failure. Include a bounded stderr tail (or the preserved failed-artifact path) in this branch as well.

src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1491-1503 and 1523-1535 now build `InvocationFailed` details from the full child stderr, and src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5058-5073 writes `error.to_string()` verbatim into `runtime/logs/run.ndjson`. With Claude `--verbose` enabled at src/adapters/process_backend.rs:777-789, a failed invocation can dump very large stderr payloads into the runtime log. The nearby comments at src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1479-1482 and 1517-1519 already acknowledge that stderr may contain echoed prompt content. This is a robustness/safety regression: a single failure can bloat `run.ndjson` and leak prompt/context into the operator log. Log only a bounded tail or a pointer to `runtime/failed/<invocation>.failed.raw` instead of the full stderr body.

In `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` lines 154 and 201, the stderr tail truncation uses a hardcoded limit of `200` bytes, while the exit-failure code paths at lines 1506 and 1544 use the `STDERR_DETAIL_LIMIT` constant (512 bytes). The schema-validation error paths should use `STDERR_DETAIL_LIMIT` instead of the hardcoded `200` so that stderr truncation is consistent across all failure paths. Currently, envelope/result parse failures show at most 200 bytes of stderr — less than half what transport failures show — which undermines the diagnostic goal of the change.

Stop truncating the `stage_failed` runtime-log error to 500 bytes in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5059-5075`, or write the full error into a separate runtime-log field. `ProcessBackendAdapter` now keeps up to 512 bytes of stderr detail for failed exits in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1501-1555` and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1851-1856`, but the engine truncates the entire `AppError` string before writing `run.ndjson`, so the newly added diagnostics are still cut off in the place operators are supposed to read them.

Add raw output sizes to the non-zero-exit error details built in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1507-1555`. The new runtime-log message in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5068-5075` relies entirely on `error.to_string()`, but the ordinary CLI failure path still logs only a stderr/stdout excerpt and omits `stdout_len`/`stderr_len` (or an equivalent raw response length).

Propagate Claude verbosity to the built-in stale-session retry path. `build_command()` only appends `--verbose` when `request.attempt_number > 1` in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:794-800`, but the retry-without-resume branch in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1422-1435` clones the original request without bumping `attempt_number` before rebuilding the command. A first-attempt resume failure therefore performs its internal retry without any extra CLI diagnostics.

In engine.rs:5066, the `error` value (AppError Display) is interpolated directly into the runtime log message string without structured field separation. If the error contains an '=' character (possible from stderr content), downstream log parsers using key=value extraction will mis-parse the line. Consider either (a) quoting the error value (e.g., `error={error:?}` to get a quoted Debug representation), or (b) moving failure_class and error into a separate structured RuntimeLogEntry with dedicated fields rather than embedding them in the free-text message.

The `stdout_error` value from `extract_stdout_error()` is included without truncation in the `detail` string at process_backend.rs:1513 and :1553. While `stderr_tail` is bounded to STDERR_DETAIL_LIMIT (512 bytes), `stdout_error` can be arbitrarily large if the `result` or `errors[]` fields in the JSON envelope are long. Apply `truncate_str` to `stdout_error` as well for consistency, e.g., `truncate_str(&out, STDERR_DETAIL_LIMIT)`.

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1511-1516` and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1552-1556` still splice `stdout_error` into the `InvocationFailed` detail without any truncation, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5065-5072` now copies that detail verbatim into `runtime/logs/run.ndjson`. `extract_stdout_error()` at `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1695-1724` can join arbitrary-length `result` / `errors[]` strings, so a single backend error can still bloat the runtime log or leak a large prompt echo even though stderr is capped. Truncate `stdout_error` to the same bound as `stderr_tail`, or replace it with a pointer to the preserved `runtime/failed/*.failed.raw` artifact.

Two decoder failure paths still miss the new diagnostics entirely: `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:227-239` (`Claude returned empty result with no structured_output`) omits any stderr detail, and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:334-340` (`failed to read codex last-message file`) omits `stdout_len` / `stderr_len`. Because `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5065-5072` only forwards `error.to_string()`, these branches still produce thin `stage_failed` runtime logs. They should attach the same bounded stderr/raw-length context, or at least the preserved failed-artifact path, that the neighboring invalid-JSON branches now include.

In engine.rs line 5028 and 5058, error.to_string() is called twice on the same error object (once for the journal event, once for the runtime log). The second call at line 5058 could reuse the string from line 5028 by hoisting the `let error_display = error.to_string();` above the journal event construction and passing `&error_display` to both. This is a minor efficiency issue but worth noting since error messages can be large (they now include stderr tails up to 512 bytes).

The Codex failure paths in process_backend.rs (lines 304-315, 322-334, 338-349) include stdout_len and stderr_len but do NOT include stderr_tail content like the Claude paths do. For consistency and to meet the acceptance criterion 'Raw backend output is preserved or logged on failure', the Codex error messages should also include a stderr_tail snippet, especially for the 'invalid Codex last-message JSON' case where the stderr may contain the actual error.

src/adapters/process_backend.rs:152-161, 199-212, 228-240, 305-345 and src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5058-5073 still do not put the malformed backend payload itself into `runtime/logs/run.ndjson`. These branches now record `stdout_len`/`stderr_len` (and sometimes a `stderr_tail`), and engine logs that string verbatim, but the actual bad stdout body from the motivating invalid-JSON case is still only available by opening `runtime/failed/<invocation>.failed.raw`. Add a bounded stdout/stderr snippet, or at least the failed-artifact path, to the logged error so `stage_failed` is actionable on its own.

In engine.rs line 5068, the runtime log format uses `error={:?}` (Debug formatting) on `error_display` which is already a Display string. This double-quotes and escape-encodes the entire error message (which now includes truncated stderr/stdout tails). With 512 bytes of stderr tail embedded inside a Debug-formatted string inside another format string, runtime log lines can easily exceed 1KB of escaped content. Consider using `error={}` (Display) or, better, emitting `error_display` and `failure_class` as separate structured fields on the RuntimeLogEntry rather than cramming everything into the `message` string. This would keep runtime log entries machine-parseable and avoid double-escaping.

In process_backend.rs around line 1468, the internal stale-session retry bumps `fresh_request.attempt_number` via `saturating_add(1)`. This means the attempt_number in the resulting InvocationFailed error no longer matches the engine's retry counter (cursor.attempt). If the engine logs or uses attempt_number from the error to correlate with its own retry state, the mismatch could cause confusion. Consider using a separate field (e.g., `internal_retry: bool` or `verbose_enabled: bool`) to control --verbose enablement rather than overloading attempt_number, which has semantic meaning for the engine's retry policy.

src/adapters/process_backend.rs:333-349 still omits the actual Codex stdout content when `--output-last-message` is missing and the stdout fallback is not valid JSON. This branch only reports `stdout_len` and `stderr_tail`, so the `stage_failed` runtime log still lacks the malformed response body for one of the main Codex JSON-parse failure modes. Add a bounded `stdout_tail` here and cover it with a regression test next to the existing Codex diagnostics tests.

src/adapters/process_backend.rs:825-830 assumes Claude retry diagnostics come from `--verbose`, but the installed Claude CLI documents `--debug` / `--debug-file` as the diagnostic switches while `--verbose` is only a config override. Re-check the CLI contract and switch to the documented debug flag, or add an integration test proving that `--verbose` really produces extra failure detail in `-p --output-format json` mode.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: plan_and_implement, result_len: 1570)

details.body: In build_command (around line 836) and the stale-session retry path (around line 1477-1478), the code unconditionally passes --verbose to Claude CLI on retries. If the installed Claude CLI version does not support --verbose (or changes its behavior), this will cause the retry invocation itself to fail with an unrecognized-flag error, making retries strictly worse. Guard this by first probing the Claude CLI version/capabilities, or catch and log unrecognized-flag errors from the retry so the retry can fall back to running without --verbose.

Include the malformed Codex last-message length in the schema-validation error details. In src/adapters/process_backend.rs:310-333, the `invalid Codex last-message JSON` branch now records `message_tail`, but it still only reports `stdout_len` and `stderr_len`. For this failure mode the raw response is `last_message_text`, not stdout, so the `stage_failed` runtime log still lacks the raw-response length for one of the main Codex parse failures the bead is supposed to diagnose. Extend this path and its regression test at src/adapters/process_backend.rs:3678-3725 to emit `message_len` (or equivalent) alongside `message_tail`.

Add a workflow-engine regression test that reads `runtime/logs/run.ndjson` after a failing stage and asserts the `stage_failed` entry includes `failure_class=` plus the propagated backend error details. The new runtime-log contract lives in src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5060-5076, but the existing workflow-engine coverage only checks unrelated runtime-log warnings at tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs:540-550 and failure/journal behavior at tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs:3165-3180 and tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs:3420-3428; it never verifies the new `stage_failed` payload itself.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: plan_and_implement, result_len: 172)

details.body: In src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs around line 5068, the runtime log message embeds error_display with a bare `error={}` format. Since error_display can contain spaces, equals signs, and other characters, downstream log parsers that split on `=` or whitespace will mis-parse the message. Consider either (a) quoting the error value (e.g., `error={error_display:?}` using Debug format), or (b) emitting failure_class and a truncated error summary as separate structured fields in RuntimeLogEntry (if the struct supports it), rather than stuffing everything into a single message string.

Add a comment in src/adapters/process_backend.rs (near the Codex build_command path) documenting the investigation outcome for Codex CLI debug flags. The bead requirement #4 explicitly asked to check whether Codex CLI has --verbose/--debug. If it doesn't have one, document that finding so future maintainers don't re-investigate. If it does, add the flag on retries analogous to the Claude path.

At `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:181-191`, the Claude zero-exit `is_error`/`BackendExhausted` path still builds an `InvocationFailed` message without `stdout_len`/`stderr_len` or any bounded raw-output tail. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5068-5074` only forwards `error_display` into the `stage_failed` runtime-log line, so this class of stage failure still does not satisfy the new requirement that runtime logs include the raw response length/details. Please add the same bounded diagnostics here and cover this path with a regression test.

In src/adapters/process_backend.rs around line 1493-1504, the stale-session retry path clones the original request (preserving attempt_number), calls build_command (which adds --verbose when attempt_number > 1), then unconditionally push_arg("--verbose") again. When attempt_number > 1, --verbose appears twice in the CLI args. Guard the push_arg with a check: only add --verbose if build_command hasn't already added it (e.g., `if fresh_request.attempt_number <= 1 { fresh_prepared.push_arg(...) }`).

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:868-875` claims Codex already emits a full NDJSON event stream, but the command builders at `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1316-1339` and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1343-1369` never pass `codex exec --json`. As written, Codex is still invoked in its default output mode, so this patch does not actually enable any richer Codex diagnostic stream for transient failures. Wire up the supported JSON/event output mode (or another real debug mechanism) before treating Codex diagnostics as handled.

In the stale-session retry path (process_backend.rs ~line 1505), the condition `fresh_request.attempt_number <= 1` is checked to decide whether to push --verbose. However, `fresh_request` is a clone of `request` — its `attempt_number` is whatever the caller set, not necessarily related to whether build_command already added --verbose. If the caller ever sets attempt_number=0 (or another unexpected value), the condition could misbehave. Consider checking `!fresh_prepared.args().contains(&"--verbose".to_owned())` directly instead, which is the actual invariant being guarded.

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:849-850` and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1505-1508` add `--verbose` to Claude retries, but the decoder still assumes stdout is the normal single-object Claude envelope (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:153-171`). I verified against the installed Claude CLI that `claude -p --output-format json --verbose ...` emits a JSON array of event objects on stdout, not the usual result envelope. That means retried Claude failures now get turned into `invalid Claude envelope JSON` schema failures, and non-zero exits also lose their real error text because the retry path still relies on `extract_stdout_error()` (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1609-1637`, `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1776-1795`), which only understands the non-verbose envelope shape. In practice this makes retryable Claude failures less diagnosable and can make the stale-session retry path fail deterministically.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: plan_and_implement, result_len: 1192)
backend invocation failed for contract 'final_review:reviewer' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: final_review:reviewer, result_len: 40)

details.body: In the non-zero exit code error paths (around lines 1563-1572 and 1608-1618 of process_backend.rs), the `detail` string already contains the stderr_tail content, and then `(stdout_len: ..., stderr_len: ...)` is appended at the end. However, `stdout_tail` is not included in these non-zero exit paths, unlike the zero-exit-code paths which include both stdout_tail and stderr_tail. For consistency and diagnostics completeness, consider also including `stdout_tail` in the non-zero exit code error messages, since stdout may contain partial JSON or other diagnostic output even when the process exits with a non-zero code.

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:100-132` preserves raw failure output as `runtime/failed/<invocation_id>.failed.raw`, but `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5060-5075` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/journal.rs:213-236` never log the `invocation_id` or failed-raw path. The artifact name also includes completion round via `history_record_base_id` in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:631-649`, so stage/cycle/attempt alone are not enough to identify the right file after a completion-round restart. Please emit the invocation id (or the exact failed artifact path) with `stage_failed`; otherwise the preserved raw output is often not discoverable when operators need it.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'final_review:reviewer' via 'claude': invalid Claude result JSON: expected value at line 1 column 1 (contract: final_review:reviewer, result_len: 76, stdout_len: 994, result_tail: "Full test suite passed (exit 0), confirming no regressions from the changes.", stderr_tail: "")
